governance.permitted_use: Research and benchmarking; private use only.

diagnostics.interpretation: Variété, espèce, lot ou condition différente mais physiquement plausible.
diagnostics.label: Spectre hors domaine valide

diagnostics.interpretation: Mélange de campagnes, opérateurs, lots, setups ou sous-populations spectrales.
diagnostics.label: Dataset multi-régimes

diagnostics.interpretation: Rupture aux jonctions de détecteurs, calibration locale ou sonde différente.
diagnostics.label: Splice / raccord détecteurs

diagnostics.interpretation: Effet systématique du support, blanc/noir, transflectance ou environnement de mesure.
diagnostics.label: Fond différent

diagnostics.interpretation: Combinaison possible changement de sonde + splice, amplifiée par géométrie, fond ou calibration.
diagnostics.label: Signature VERA25-like

diagnostics.interpretation: Couverture partielle du spot; contribution du fond ou du support.
diagnostics.label: Mélange feuille + fond

diagnostics.interpretation: Artefacts numériques ou traitement spectral incorrect.
diagnostics.label: Erreur interpolation / rééchantillonnage

diagnostics.interpretation: Décalage systématique entre campagnes, instruments ou référence blanche.
diagnostics.label: Erreur calibration / référence blanche

A very fine-grained, porphyritic rock, light gray in color.Geologic age - Tertiary (Post-Oligocene).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.alkalic.solid.ward17.spectrum.txt

A gray, medium-grained rock spotted with black prismaticcrystals.Geologic age - Post-Oligocene.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.alkalic.solid.ward18.spectrum.txt

A granular gray rock composed of feldspar showing goodcleavage and considerable hornblende.Geologic age - Post-Carboniferous - Pre-Cretaceous.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.alkalic.solid.ward19.spectrum.txt

A gray granular rock composed of feldspar showing twinning,nepheline, sodalite and hornblende. The sodalite is grayish to bluish-gray and can easily be distinguised in the hand specimen by its orange fluorscence in ultra-violet light. Geologic age - Late Devonian or late Carboniferous.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.alkalic.solid.ward23.spectrum.txt

A cellular lava rock that varies in color from reddish brownto brownish black. Tiny glassy crystals of feldspar can sometimes bediscerned with the aid of a hand lens.Geologic age - Pleistocene.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.basic.solid.ward02.spectrum.txt

A purplish, compact rock with innumerable amygdules of varyingsize filled largely with quartz, epidote, calcite, and chlorite.Geologic Age - Pre-Cambrian.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.basic.solid.ward34.spectrum.txt

A jet black glass with conchoidal fracture. Thin edgesometimes show parallel flow bands. Geologic age - Tertiary.Geologic Name - Glass Buttes Series.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward01.spectrum.txt

A light-gray felsitic rock, somewhat porous and earthy inappearance, with speckles of pyrite.Geologic age - Tertiary (Oligocene).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward04.spectrum.txt

A dense felsitic rock varying in color from salmon to red.The original flow structure is often apparent.Geologic age - Devonian or Carboniferous. Geologic name - Mattapan Felsite.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward05.spectrum.txt

A pinkish rock with scattered phenocrysts of glassy quartzin a felsitic and somewhat vesicular groundmass.Geologic age - Tertiary (Eocene).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward07.spectrum.txt

A dark gray, compact, porphyritic rock with phenocrysts offeldspar and quartz in a cryptocrystalline groundmass.Geologic age - Devonian or Carboniferous.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward09.spectrum.txt

A gray, medium-grained rock consisting of feldspar, quartz,biotite and muscovite.Geologic age - Devonian.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward11.spectrum.txt

A light-colored medium-grained rock composed of feldspar,quartz, muscovite and biotite.Geologic age - Late Paleozoic.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward12.spectrum.txt

A dark gray granular rock consisting of feldspar, quartz andblack pryibole.Geologic age - Carboniferous (probably Mississippian).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward13.spectrum.txt

A gray, granular rock consisting of feldspar, quartz andhornblende.Geologic age - Carboniferous (probably Mississippian).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward14.spectrum.txt

A light gray graphic granite composed chiefly of perthiteand smoky quartz with some muscovite.Geologic age - Late Devonian (?).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward15.spectrum.txt

A fine-grained pink rock composed of quartz, glassy feldsparand biotite. The hand specimen occasionally has irregular stringers andpatches of coarser grain.Geologic age - Pre-Cambrian.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward16.spectrum.txt

A gray, prophyritic rock, occasionally vesicular, prominentphenocrysts of smoky quartz averaging 5mm in width. Subhedral to euhedralphenocrysts of glassy feldspar averaging 4mm in length. Tiny garnet andtopaz crystals in some vesicles.Geologic age - Tertiary (Eocene).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.felsic.solid.ward08.spectrum.txt

A purplish gray rock consisting chiefly of plagioclase,orthoclase, and greenish hornblende with small amounts of quartz.Geologic age - Tertiary (Miocene).Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.ward24.spectrum.txt

A gray pseudo-porphyritic rock. Phenocrysts of pink feldsparmay be an inch long. Smaller phenocrysts of white feldspar, quartz,hornblende and mica are in a granular groundmass.Geologic age - Tertiary.Collected by: Ward's Natural Science Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jpl.perkin.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.ward25.spectrum.txt
